modelDispatch.rationale: Adrian directive 2026-05-04 — feedback_no_haiku_dispatch.md. Haiku shipped real defects (cache-first SW, wrong-framework imports, fabricated input files, invalid token paths) faster than the gates could catch.

concurrency.rationale: Mario Zechner: parallel sub-agents are an anti-pattern when they share files. Cross-pod sibling-patches blurred ownership in batches 1–2.

fileOverlap.rationale: Worktree isolation reduces but doesn't eliminate cross-contamination. When two pods edit the same file path, the merge produces commits whose messages don't match contents (observed in batch 1).

uncommittedHumanWork.rationale: If Adrian has uncommitted edits, an agent must not touch those files. Avoids stomping on in-progress human work.

visualUI.rationale: Visual debt the gates couldn't catch in batch 3: PWA cache-first SW, segmented-control overflow, routes-tree styling. All required Adrian eyeballs. Cost > savings on every dispatch.

selfReferentialDispatch.rationale: 13g-8 modifies hermes-unit.mjs — must dispatch via Claude Code Agent tool (different runner layer), not via those same scripts.

dispatchableSafeUnattended.rationale: Default safeForUnattended=false. Each unit explicitly marks true. Conservative; new gaps surface as deferred-not-dispatched, never as runaway.

fixtureDriftWindow.rationale: Gates that haven't fired in 90 days are either dormant or perfectly clean — surface for review, don't auto-park.
